title: game studio pipelines concept to build
purpose: F3.2 defines game studio pipelines concept to build for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling.
scope: Owns `F3.2` only; siblings under `F3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).

Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §8 — Branch F — Organization plane (template-packs = ceiling)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#8-branch-f-organization-plane-template-packs-ceiling).
behavior_steps: At H1 plan approval for a game-studio goal, program-scoper binds the active pack's `pipelines/*.yaml` so pursuit phases follow concept → mesh → rig → animation → engine import → QA → build in enforced order.

Each phase turn loads role-specific playbooks ([F1.6](F1.6-pack-playbooks-role-specific.md)) and seeded task cards ([F1.7](F1.7-pack-template-tasks-seed-cards.md)) so implement workers advance only the current pipeline gate—not downstream animator or build work.
The integration manifest declares which asset phases may parallelize; the conductor schedules lane workstreams without violating ordering gates (e.g. no animator tasks before rig evidence passes).

QA evidence must pass before build-phase tasks enqueue; partial phase completion rolls up in state but does not unlock ship until [F3.4](F3.4-game-studio-goal-verify-asset-engine-tests-perf.md) atop [F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md) succeeds.
If a worker attempts an out-of-order phase, required prior gate evidence is missing, or parallel lanes collide on the same asset lease, pursuit halts at H2 until the conductor reorders work at H1.

Edge case `F3.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `F3.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Per-role allowed_reads in lane.json work orders.
Validate `F3.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `F3.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `F3.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: F3.2 defines game studio pipelines concept to build for the agent-driven expert system. Organization — template-packs as whole-company ceiling. In practice, the system at h1 plan approval for a game-studio goal, program-scoper binds the active pack's `pipelines/*.yaml` so pursuit phases follow concept → mesh → rig → animation → engine import → qa → build in enforced order, then each phase turn loads role-specific playbooks ([f1.6](f1.6-pack-playbooks-role-specific.md)) and seeded task cards ([f1.7](f1.7-pack-template-tasks-seed-cards.md)) so implement workers advance only the current pipeline gate—not downstream animator or build work.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-f 2026-06-28 -->

Game studio pipelines walk concept → mesh → rig → animation → engine import → QA → build—each phase bound to roles and gates in pipelines/*.yaml. Phase order prevents animator work before rig exists and blocks build before QA evidence passes.

Pipeline phases reference pack playbooks ([F1.6](F1.6-pack-playbooks-role-specific.md)) and seed task cards ([F1.7](F1.7-pack-template-tasks-seed-cards.md)) for implement turns. Integration manifest declares which phases may parallelize (e.g. multiple asset lanes). goal_verify at ship uses [F3.4](F3.4-game-studio-goal-verify-asset-engine-tests-perf.md) suite atop generic [F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md).
